# Menu Optimization for Coffee Shops

## 1. Pricing Strategy

### Cost-Plus Pricing
Standard markup: **3–4x cost of goods** for beverages, **2–2.5x** for food items.

| Item | COGS | Suggested Price | Margin |
|---|---|---|---|
| Espresso drink | $0.80–1.20 | $3.50–5.50 | 75–80% |
| Drip coffee | $0.20–0.40 | $2.00–3.50 | 85–90% |
| Pastry | $1.50–2.50 | $3.50–5.00 | 50–60% |
| Sandwich | $2.50–4.00 | $7.00–10.00 | 55–65% |

### Psychological Pricing
- Charm pricing ($4.95 vs $5.00) works for food, less for coffee
- **Anchor pricing**: Place a premium item ($7.50 specialty latte) to make $5.00 standard drinks seem reasonable
- Menu position: Items in the upper-right corner of a menu get **15–20% more orders**

## 2. Menu Engineering

### The BCG Matrix for Menu Items
| Category | High Popularity | Low Popularity |
|---|---|---|
| **High Margin** | Stars (promote) | Puzzles (reposition) |
| **Low Margin** | Plowhorses (re-price) | Dogs (remove) |

### Optimal Menu Size
- **12–18 drink items** is optimal. Beyond 20, decision fatigue reduces average spend
- Food menu: **6–10 items** maximum for a coffee-focused shop
- Rotate **2–3 seasonal specials** to drive repeat visits

## 3. Seasonal Items

- **Seasonal drinks** generate 15–25% higher margins (perceived exclusivity)
- Pumpkin spice (Oct–Nov), peppermint (Dec), lavender (Spring), cold brew variants (Summer)
- Seasonal items should represent **15–20% of total menu** at any given time
- **Risk**: Seasonal changeover costs (new ingredients, training, marketing materials)

## 4. Upselling and Add-ons

- "Would you like an extra shot?" — average upsell value: $0.75, acceptance rate: 20–30%
- Add-on margins: Extra shot ($0.15 cost, $0.75 price = 80% margin)
- Loyalty programs increase visit frequency by **25–35%** and average ticket by **10–15%**

## 5. Cost Control

- **Food waste target**: Below 5% of COGS
- **Pour consistency**: Standardized recipes reduce variance in COGS by 10–15%
- Weekly COGS review: Flag any item where actual cost exceeds planned cost by >10%
